name: Betaine anhydrous
summary: Decision-ready summary: Betaine anhydrous is best framed for homocysteine reduction; methylation support; power output context; safety screen: kidney disease; elevated LDL concern in some users.
description: Betaine anhydrous is used for methyl-donor biology, potentially influencing homocysteine metabolism via betaine-mediated remethylation and hepatic nutrient handling.
safetyNotes: Human intervention data were limited and did not establish safety at the applicant's proposed 2.5 g/day novel-food use. Available studies up to 4 g/day for several months did not show platelet-count effects, but long-term, pregnancy, lactation, kidney-disease, and interaction evidence remains insufficient.
